title: Economic profile: Claresholm, Alberta - Canada.ca
content:
## Economic profile: Claresholm, Alberta
Claresholm Footnote 1 has a population of 3,804, consisting of 36% seniors (65 and older), 51% working-age adults (15 to 64) and 13% children (under 15).
The key sectors of the economy in Claresholm, which account for 53% of the jobs in the region, are
- health care and social assistance
- accommodation and food services
There are 1,435 employed residents, of which 9.2% are immigrants. Footnote 2
The economy generates a total of $194 million in household and business incomes, which amounts to $50,895 per resident.

### General age structure – 2021
% children (under 15)
% working-age adults (15 to 64)
% of seniors (65 and older)
In order to support local services such as health care and social programs and maintain a good standard of living for everyone in the region, it is essential to have more working-age adults than seniors. Claresholm has fewer working-age adults than seniors compared to the average in Alberta.
From 2011 to 2021, Claresholm experienced
- a 1.2% increase in population, compared to a 17% increase in the province
- a 26% increase in the population’s proportion of seniors, compared to a 33% increase in the province
- an 11% decrease in the population’s proportion of working-age adults, compared to a 5.5% decrease in the province

### Population structure – Changes from 2011 to 2021
Changes in the region
Changes in the province
Proportion of children
Proportion of working-age adults
Proportion of seniors
By 2031, without immigration, Footnote 3 the region could experience Footnote 4
- a 12% decrease in population
- a 5.2% decrease in the population’s proportion of working-age adults, which could limit the availability and quality of local services
- a further increase in the population’s proportion of seniors by 12%, potentially changing the demand for local services, including health care and long-term care

### No immigration – Possible changes from 2021 to 2031
Changes in the region
Changes in the province
Proportion of children
Proportion of working-age adults
Proportion of seniors
The region relies heavily on working-age adults to keep local services running smoothly and to maintain a good standard of living and economic growth. Immigrants can help by filling job gaps and bringing new skills and ideas, which keeps the local economy strong.
From 2011 to 2021, Claresholm experienced
- an 8.6% decrease in workforce Footnote 5 size, compared to an 8.5% increase in the province
- a 26% increase in the number of immigrant workers, compared to a 42% increase in the province
- a total of 461 retirements, Footnote 6 which amounts to 29% of the 2021 workforce, compared to 15% in the province
- a change in the average income per resident from local jobs relative to the province, which is 18% lower than the average in Alberta in 2021

By 2031, without immigration, Footnote 8 the region could experience Footnote 9
- a further decrease in workforce size by 44%
- a 40% decrease in the number of jobs
- an increase in the proportion of seniors in the population from 36% to 40%
